user_query:
I’m a B2B PLG SaaS founder. I want a LinkedIn post to drive warm DMs for our “customer stories” pilot. I’ve put quantified context in input/context.json.

Please follow a strict three-phase workflow and write ONLY to files under output/:

Phase 1 — Strategic Interview:
- Read input/context.json.
- Draft 10–12 sharp interview questions to close any gaps before writing. Number them 1. to 12. Keep them direct and specific (cover: target audience, starting situation, business goal & CTA, exact before→after numbers with timeframe, 2–3 step mechanism, counter‑intuitive insight, credibility/costly signals, constraints/risks, what’s publicly claimable, offer details). Do not write the post yet.
- After the questions, add a “Validation checklist:” block with four lines exactly in this format, each marked Yes/No based on input/context.json:
  - Metric: Yes/No
  - Insight: Yes/No
  - Mechanism: Yes/No
  - CTA: Yes/No
- Save as output/phase1_questions.txt

Phase 2 — Hook Engineering:
- Propose 4 single‑line hooks only, no explanations. Number them 1. to 4.
- Rules: no rhetorical questions, use radical specificity with numbers, reveal 80% (result), hide 20% (method), no fluff. Keep each as one concise line.
- Save as output/hooks.txt

Phase 3 — Post Body:
- Read input/chosen_hook.json to select which hook number to use.
- Write the full LinkedIn post body and save as output/post.txt
- Requirements:
  - Start with the exact chosen hook text (no preamble).
  - Use ABT structure (And–But–Therefore) with short, mobile‑first paragraphs (no line longer than 140 characters).
  - Active voice throughout; no rhetorical questions.
  - Remove filler words: very, really, incredibly.
  - Include a costly signal from the context (time/money/effort) and at least one concrete number.
  - End with a clear, directive CTA telling readers to DM “STORY” to get the SOP (not a question).
  - Use minimal Unicode bold for emphasis if needed.

Do not include any meta commentary in the files. Read input/context.json and input/chosen_hook.json and write the three files exactly as specified.
